dependencyDashboardHeader:
## How to use this dashboard Full playbook: [RENOVATE_STRATEGY.md](../blob/main/.github/RENOVATE_STRATEGY.md) **Quick section reference** - **Pending Approval**: tick a checkbox to open the PR. Only tick items worth trying. - **Awaiting Schedule** / **Rate-Limited**: automatic, no action needed. - **Pending Status Checks**: should stay empty. Renovate PRs open immediately so CI runs on the PR itself. - **PR Closed (Blocked)**: previously closed. Only tick to retry. - **Detected Dependencies**: reference only.
Automerge policy | Dependency type | Auto/Manual | Release age | Commit type | Triggers release? | | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | | devDependencies (patch/minor/major) | Auto | 3 days | `chore(dev-deps)` | No | | TypeScript (major) | Manual | 3 days | `chore(tooling)` | No | | Production deps patch/minor (`packages/core`, `packages/react`) | Auto | 3 days | `fix(deps)` | **Yes, patch** | | Production deps major (`packages/core`, `packages/react`) | Manual | 3 days | `fix(deps)` | **Yes, patch**
(override to `feat!` at squash if breaking) | | Peer deps | Manual | 3 days | `chore(peer-deps)` | No | | App deps (`apps/**`) | Auto | 3 days | `chore(apps)` | No | | High-severity security | Manual | 0 days | varies | varies | | Trusted upstream (Sanity-maintained, React, Next, `@types/*`, etc.; full list in [preset](https://github.com/sanity-io/renovate-config/blob/main/min-age-3days.json)) | inherits | **0 days** | inherits | inherits |
Common actions **Retry a closed PR**: find it under "PR Closed (Blocked)" and tick the box. **Approve a major dep update**: tick its checkbox under "Pending Approval". This only opens the PR; you still review and squash-merge manually. If the upgrade is a breaking change for our consumers, change the commit type from `fix(deps)` to `feat(deps)!` at squash time so release-please produces a major/minor bump. **A PR won't automerge**: check CI status, that the PR is approved, and that it's not labeled `needs-review`, `major-update`, or `breaking-change`. **A lockfile looks broken**: run `pnpm install` locally, commit, and push to the Renovate branch. Renovate won't overwrite manual commits. **Rate-limited queue is huge**: drain it by merging open Renovate PRs. The throttles are `prHourlyLimit` (currently 5, the creation rate) and `prConcurrentLimit` (currently 20, the open-PR ceiling) in `.github/renovate.json`; raise either temporarily if needed. **Bumping the repo's pnpm version**: also update `constraints.pnpm` in `.github/renovate.json` to match.
Weekly checklist - Check this dashboard for pending approvals. - Review any failed automerge PRs. - Merge the release-please PR if ready. - Note any major updates that need planning.

packageRules.description: Pin groq and @sanity/codegen to the typegen-experimental prerelease tag. The kitchensink's typegen depends on APIs (defineProjection / defineQuery, multi-schema/projections) that only exist on this prerelease; do not let Renovate move them to a stable release, which would drop the experimental typegen the SDK relies on. Revisit once typegen lands in a regular release.
Runtime deps of core/react (patch/minor) - trigger patch release (automerge inherited from global). KEEP IN SYNC with core+react dependencies (manual; see RENOVATE_STRATEGY.md).

Runtime deps of core/react (major) - manual review (dependencyDashboardApproval + needs-review label gate it), still patch release by default (override to feat! at squash if breaking). KEEP IN SYNC with core+react dependencies (manual; see RENOVATE_STRATEGY.md).
Use bump strategy for the core/react runtime deps so release-please sees the version change. KEEP IN SYNC with the runtime fix-list above.
React peer dependencies - manual review (dependencyDashboardApproval + needs-review label gate it). Matched by name because catalog-flattened depType won't match peerDependencies.
App-only dependencies (not consumed by published packages) - never trigger a release.
TypeScript major versions - require approval due to breaking changes
